services.name: AI Content Strategy & Production
services.tagline: Build a content engine that runs at scale without a full creative team
services.description: You set up and manage an AI-powered content system that produces consistent, on-brand content across channels — blog, social, email, and ads. This isn't content generated and dumped into a Google Doc. It's a documented process: brand voice training, editorial calendar, prompt library, review workflow, and publishing cadence. The client gets a content operation they can hand off to a junior coordinator within 90 days.
services.ideal_client: B2B companies with 10–200 employees that are publishing inconsistently or spending $8,000+ per month on a content agency. Also works well for e-commerce brands scaling product descriptions and email flows.
deliverables: Brand voice document and AI prompt library (customized to client)
Monthly editorial calendar (30–60 pieces across formats)
Blog post production (4–8 posts/month, fully written and formatted)
Social media content (20–40 posts/month with captions and hooks)
Email newsletter production (4 emails/month)
Monthly performance report with AI-assisted analysis
Content repurposing workflow (1 blog → multiple formats)
services.timeline: Setup in weeks 1–2; full production begins week 3; month 2 onward is steady-state
services.how_to_scope: Start by auditing what they currently publish and what's working. Count their channels. If they need more than 60 pieces/month or have 4+ channels, price at the $4,500–$5,000 range. Fewer than 3 channels and under 30 pieces/month, start at $2,000–$2,500. Add $500–$1,000 if they need SEO keyword research layered in.
common_pitfalls: Not building a review step — AI content without human QA will eventually embarrass the client
Promising 'unlimited' output without defining what counts as a deliverable
Skipping brand voice training and just using the client's website as context — it's not enough
Not documenting the process from day one, making yourself a bottleneck instead of building a system
tools_typically_used: Airtable or Notion for editorial calendar
services.upsell_path: AI Marketing Analytics & Reporting (service 5) pairs naturally once content is running

services.name: AI Customer Service Implementation
services.tagline: Deflect 40–70% of support tickets before they reach a human
services.description: You implement an AI-powered customer service system — typically a chatbot plus automated email triage — trained on the client's actual support data, knowledge base, and product documentation. The goal is measurable ticket deflection. Most clients are paying $4,000–$12,000/month on support staff or a support outsourcing firm. This service should pay for itself within 60–90 days.
services.ideal_client: E-commerce brands getting 200+ support tickets/month. SaaS companies with repetitive onboarding questions. Service businesses with high inquiry volume (real estate, legal, healthcare adjacent). Any company where support is a cost center they're trying to reduce.
deliverables: Support ticket audit and categorization (what's automatable vs. what needs humans)
Knowledge base cleanup and structuring (or creation from scratch)
AI chatbot configuration and training on client-specific data
Automated email triage with routing rules
Escalation path design (what triggers a human handoff)
Staff training on monitoring and maintaining the system
30-day post-launch monitoring and tuning
Monthly performance report (deflection rate, CSAT, response time)
services.timeline: 4–6 weeks for setup. Ongoing management from month 2.
services.how_to_scope: Get the client's monthly ticket volume and average handle time. Calculate what they're currently spending on support labor. Price the setup at 15–25% of their annual support spend — this makes the ROI argument obvious. If they have a messy or nonexistent knowledge base, add $1,500–$2,500 for knowledge base work. Monthly retainer covers monitoring, retraining, and new FAQ additions.
common_pitfalls: Deploying without enough training data — the bot will hallucinate or give wrong answers
Not designing the escalation path, leading to customer frustration
Underestimating knowledge base cleanup time — this is often the biggest time sink
Promising a specific deflection rate before you've audited ticket data
services.upsell_path: AI Workflow Automation Audit (service 7) once support is running — there are always adjacent processes to automate

services.name: AI Sales Automation Setup
services.tagline: Build a sales system that researches, sequences, and follows up without manual effort
services.engagement_type: Project (with optional retainer for ongoing management)
services.description: You design and implement an AI-powered sales automation system covering prospecting, personalized outreach, follow-up sequences, and CRM enrichment. The deliverable isn't just a configured tool — it's a documented sales playbook that the client's team can run, with AI doing the research and drafting work so reps focus on conversations, not data entry.
services.ideal_client: B2B companies with a defined ICP and a sales team that's manually doing a lot of research and email writing. Typically $1M–$20M revenue range. Founder-led sales teams moving to a dedicated sales hire. Also strong for outbound-heavy agencies.
deliverables: ICP definition workshop and documentation
Prospect research automation setup (AI-enriched lead lists)
CRM audit and cleanup (or setup if they don't have one)
AI-powered email sequence templates (5–8 touch sequence)
Personalization variable framework (company-specific hooks)
CRM workflow automation (lead routing, task creation, follow-up triggers)
Sales team training (2-hour session + SOPs)
30-day QA and optimization
services.timeline: 4–6 weeks from kickoff to fully operational
services.how_to_scope: Base price at $4,000 for a single-channel (email only) setup for a team under 5 reps. Add $1,000–$1,500 per additional channel (LinkedIn, SMS). Add $1,500 if CRM needs to be set up from scratch. Enterprise or 10+ rep teams start at $7,500. The ceiling is $10,000 for multi-channel, multi-team with custom integrations.
common_pitfalls: Building automation before validating the ICP — garbage in, garbage out
Overpromising reply rates — position it as process improvement, not magic
Not documenting the system so the client team can maintain it independently
Ignoring deliverability — cold email automation without warming will get emails marked as spam
tools_typically_used: ChatGPT or Claude for sequence writing
services.upsell_path: AI Marketing Analytics & Reporting (service 5) to close the loop on what's converting

services.name: AI Document Processing & Automation
services.tagline: Eliminate manual document work with AI extraction, classification, and routing
services.description: You build AI-powered pipelines that process documents automatically — contracts, invoices, applications, forms, reports. Instead of humans reading, extracting, and re-entering data, AI does the parsing and routes outputs to the right systems. This is high-ROI work because you're replacing hours of repetitive labor with a system that runs 24/7.
services.ideal_client: Law firms, accounting firms, real estate offices, logistics companies, insurance agencies, HR departments. Basically any business that has staff spending more than 5 hours per week manually processing documents.
deliverables: Document workflow audit (what's being processed, by whom, at what volume)
Document classification and extraction system (reads and parses specified document types)
Data extraction templates for each document type
Integration with target systems (CRM, ERP, spreadsheets, cloud storage)
Validation rules and exception handling (what happens when confidence is low)
Staff training and documentation
Testing with real documents before go-live
services.timeline: 3–5 weeks depending on document complexity and number of integrations
services.how_to_scope: Count the document types and volume. Calculate current manual hours per week × hourly cost. Price at 2–4x the monthly labor savings for the setup. Low complexity (1–2 document types, simple extraction) starts at $3,000. High complexity (5+ document types, multiple system integrations, exception handling) reaches $7,000–$8,000.
common_pitfalls: Not testing with edge cases — real documents are messier than samples
Skipping exception handling — the system will fail on unusual documents and you need a plan
Underestimating integration complexity with legacy systems
Not accounting for document volume spikes in pricing (month-end, tax season, etc.)
services.upsell_path: AI Workflow Automation Audit (service 7) — once documents are flowing, there are usually downstream processes to automate too

services.name: AI Marketing Analytics & Reporting
services.tagline: Replace 10-hour monthly reporting with AI-generated insight packages delivered in 30 minutes
services.description: You set up an AI-powered analytics and reporting system that pulls data from all marketing channels, identifies patterns and anomalies, generates written commentary, and produces consistent reports for stakeholders. The client goes from spending 10–20 hours monthly on reporting to reviewing a polished package in an hour.
services.ideal_client: Marketing teams at $2M–$50M companies running multiple paid channels. Marketing agencies managing reports for multiple clients. E-commerce brands with complex attribution questions. Any company where the CMO or founder is spending meaningful time on reporting instead of strategy.
deliverables: Data source audit and connection setup (ad platforms, GA4, CRM, email)
Unified dashboard build (Looker Studio, Power BI, or Databox)
AI-generated weekly performance summary (written narrative, not just charts)
Monthly AI analysis report with recommendations
Anomaly detection and alerting setup
Attribution model configuration
Quarterly strategy review document
services.timeline: Setup in weeks 1–3; reporting cadence begins week 4
services.how_to_scope: Count their data sources and report recipients. Base price ($2,000) covers up to 5 data sources and monthly reporting. Add $500 per additional major platform. Add $1,000 for weekly cadence. $4,000 is appropriate for 10+ data sources, weekly reporting, and multiple stakeholder formats (board-level vs. tactical).
common_pitfalls: Data source connections breaking — always build with error notifications
Over-engineering the dashboard before understanding what decisions it needs to support
Not getting stakeholder buy-in on the report format before building
Using AI to generate commentary without human review — occasional interpretations are wrong
tools_typically_used: ChatGPT or Claude for narrative generation
services.upsell_path: AI Content Strategy (service 1) — if you're reporting on what's working, you can also produce it

services.name: AI Chatbot Development & Deployment
services.tagline: Custom AI chatbots that actually know your business — not generic FAQ bots
services.description: You design, build, and deploy custom AI chatbots for specific use cases: lead qualification, customer support, internal knowledge retrieval, onboarding, or sales assistance. The differentiator here vs. plug-and-play tools is deep customization — trained on client-specific data, connected to their systems, and designed around actual user journeys rather than a generic flow.
services.ideal_client: Companies with high website traffic that aren't converting enough leads. SaaS products needing better in-app support. Large teams needing internal knowledge retrieval. Real estate, insurance, financial services, legal — any sector with complex qualification or lookup needs.
deliverables: Use case definition and conversation design document
Knowledge base preparation and chunking
Chatbot build (custom or on platform — Voiceflow, Botpress, or custom API)
Integration with CRM, calendar, helpdesk, or other systems
Conversation flow testing and QA
Analytics and conversation monitoring setup
Staff handover documentation
30-day post-launch monitoring and optimization
services.timeline: Simple bots: 3–4 weeks. Complex multi-intent bots with integrations: 6–10 weeks.
services.how_to_scope: Start with a use case definition session. Count the number of intents (things users want to do). Base price ($5,000) covers a single use case (e.g., lead capture) with up to 3 system integrations. Complex bots with 10+ intents, multiple integrations, or real-time data lookups run $10,000–$15,000. Custom API development adds $2,000–$5,000.
common_pitfalls: Building too many flows before testing core flows with real users
Underestimating knowledge base preparation time
Not designing a graceful fallback when the bot can't answer
Ignoring mobile experience — many chatbot platforms render poorly on mobile
services.upsell_path: AI Customer Service Implementation (service 2) to extend the chatbot into a full support system

services.name: AI Workflow Automation Audit & Implementation
services.tagline: Find the 10 hours per week hiding in manual processes and automate them
services.description: You start with a structured automation audit — mapping how work actually flows through the business and identifying where AI can eliminate manual steps. Then you build. This is a discovery-led service that positions you as a strategic partner, not just a developer. Clients who've had this done correctly often say it's the most valuable engagement they've done in years.
services.ideal_client: Operations-heavy businesses: agencies, law firms, accounting firms, logistics companies, real estate teams, e-commerce operators. Any company with 10+ employees where the answer to 'why do you do it that way?' is 'we've always done it that way.'
deliverables: 3-hour workflow mapping workshop (remote or in-person)
Automation opportunity report (ranked by time savings × implementation effort)
Priority automation builds (3–5 workflows depending on scope)
Before/after time tracking documentation
Implementation documentation and SOPs
Team training on maintaining automations
30-day check-in call
services.timeline: Audit: 1 week. Implementation: 2–4 weeks depending on automation count and complexity.
services.how_to_scope: Sell the audit first at $1,500–$2,500 (included in the full project price if they proceed). This de-risks the engagement for both sides. Full implementation pricing depends on automation count: 3 automations = $2,500–$3,500; 5+ automations = $4,500–$6,000. Complex integrations with APIs or legacy systems add cost.
common_pitfalls: Starting with implementation before completing the audit — you'll build the wrong things
Automating broken processes instead of fixing them first
Building fragile automations that break when data formats change
Not documenting automations properly, making the client dependent on you for every change
tools_typically_used: Custom Python scripts for complex logic
services.upsell_path: Fractional AI Officer (service 9) to continue the relationship strategically after project completion

services.name: AI Training & Enablement
services.tagline: Get your entire team using AI effectively within 30 days — not just the tech-savvy few
services.description: You design and deliver an AI training program tailored to the client's industry, tools, and team composition. Not a generic 'intro to ChatGPT' session — role-specific training that shows each team member exactly how AI applies to their daily work. Marketing gets prompt libraries for campaigns. Sales gets sequences and research workflows. Operations gets automation guides. Everyone gets a reason to adopt.
services.ideal_client: Companies with 10–150 employees starting an AI initiative. Professional services firms (accounting, law, consulting). Marketing and creative agencies. Any organization where leadership has mandated AI adoption but hasn't provided a roadmap.
deliverables: Team assessment (survey to understand current AI usage and comfort levels)
Custom training curriculum design (role-specific modules)
Live workshop delivery (half-day or full-day, remote or in-person)
Role-specific prompt libraries (one per department/function)
Tool setup guidance for each team member
Post-training implementation guide (30-day adoption plan)
Follow-up Q&A session at week 3
Slack/Teams channel template for ongoing AI sharing
services.timeline: Curriculum design: 1–2 weeks. Workshop delivery: 1 day. Follow-up: 4 weeks post-workshop.
services.how_to_scope: Base price ($3,000) covers a half-day workshop for up to 15 people with standard prompt libraries. Full-day workshop with custom role-specific content and follow-up program: $5,000–$6,000. Enterprise with custom LMS integration, manager certification program, or multi-session delivery: $7,000–$8,000.
common_pitfalls: Delivering generic training not tied to their actual tools and workflows
No follow-up — adoption falls off a cliff without a 30-day reinforcement plan
Training everyone the same way when a content writer and an accountant need completely different things
Skipping the assessment and building curriculum blind
services.upsell_path: Fractional AI Officer (service 9) to maintain strategic oversight of their AI program long-term

services.name: Fractional AI Officer
services.tagline: A Chief AI Officer without the $250K salary — strategic AI leadership embedded in your business
services.description: You function as the client's embedded AI strategist — attending leadership meetings, evaluating AI tool investments, training the team, managing AI vendor relationships, and building a multi-year AI roadmap. This is your highest-leverage retainer because you're not delivering tasks; you're shaping direction. Best sold after completing a project engagement where you've proven your strategic value.
services.ideal_client: Companies between $2M–$50M revenue that know AI is strategically important but don't have the internal expertise to lead it. Often sold to a CEO or COO who is personally driving the AI initiative but can't dedicate enough time to it. Also strong for PE-backed portfolio companies implementing AI across holdings.
deliverables: Monthly AI strategy review and roadmap update
AI tool evaluation and procurement support (unbiased vendor assessment)
Team AI capability assessments and training coordination
AI project oversight and QA
Vendor and partner relationship management
Board or leadership AI update presentations (quarterly)
AI policy and governance documentation
Weekly async update + monthly 90-minute strategy call
services.timeline: Ongoing. Minimum 3-month commitment recommended. Value compounds over time as you build institutional knowledge.
services.how_to_scope: Price based on engagement intensity. $3,000/month = 4–6 hours/month (strategy calls + async review). $5,000–$6,000/month = 8–12 hours (active project oversight + strategy). $7,000–$8,000/month = 15–20 hours (embedded in leadership rhythm, attending team meetings, real-time availability). Always require a 3-month minimum; 6-month recommended.
common_pitfalls: Not defining what decisions require your input vs. what the client can do independently
Creeping into implementation work when you're positioned as strategic — this devalues the retainer
Not documenting your work, making it hard to show value at renewal
Taking on too many Fractional AI Officer clients — your credibility depends on being genuinely engaged
tools_typically_used: Linear or Asana for project oversight
services.upsell_path: Any implementation service (1–7) when a strategic need gets identified through the advisory relationship

services.name: AI Agent Deployment
services.tagline: Custom agentic workflows that complete multi-step tasks autonomously — not just answer questions
services.engagement_type: Project (with optional $2,000–$5,000/month for hosting, monitoring, and iteration)
services.description: You design and build autonomous AI agents that complete complex, multi-step tasks without human intervention. This is the frontier of what AI can do operationally: research agents that compile competitive intelligence daily, agents that monitor inboxes and draft responses for approval, agents that manage project task creation and assignment, or agents that run entire outbound prospecting cycles. This is your premium flagship service.
services.ideal_client: Tech-forward companies with a clear, high-value repetitive process they want automated end-to-end. B2B SaaS companies. Venture-backed startups with operational bottlenecks. Companies that have already automated simple workflows (Tier 1) and are ready to go deeper.
deliverables: Agent design session (defining the goal, inputs, outputs, decision boundaries)
Tool and data access architecture
Agent build and testing (custom code or low-code depending on complexity)
Human-in-the-loop controls and approval workflows where appropriate
Monitoring and alerting setup
Deployment to production environment
Runbook documentation (how the agent works, how to modify it, what to do when it fails)
30-day post-launch support and iteration
services.timeline: Simple agents (single task, limited tools): 3–5 weeks. Complex multi-agent systems: 8–14 weeks.
services.how_to_scope: Start with an agent scoping session ($1,500–$2,500, credited toward the project). Price based on: number of distinct tasks the agent must complete, number of external systems it must interact with, degree of autonomy required, compliance and security requirements. Single-task agent with 2–3 integrations: $8,000–$12,000. Multi-agent system with 5+ integrations and human-in-the-loop controls: $18,000–$25,000.
common_pitfalls: Building without defining the failure boundary — what does the agent do when it's unsure?
Not including human-in-the-loop checkpoints for high-stakes actions
Underestimating infrastructure and hosting costs
Skipping the scoping session and building based on a vague brief
tools_typically_used: Supabase or Postgres for state management
services.upsell_path: Fractional AI Officer (service 9) to oversee the growing agentic infrastructure strategically
